\caption{
\textbf{Linear scaling of the Initiative suppressor on the v7\_pf3 factor solution.}
Factor-score $\Delta = \hat{F}(\text{LoRA}) - \hat{F}(\text{baseline})$, in $\sigma$-units, per factor, on the same 200-persona prefix-stable stratified subsample (seed=436).
95\% percentile bootstrap CI in brackets. The target factor (\textbf{F0\_Initiative}) is bolded.
All three runs use the same v7 fc\_pair questionnaire and the same k=4 oblimin factor solution (factors: F0~Initiative, F1~Pedagogy, F2~Warmth, F3~Hedging) — distinct from the main paper's v5 solution; included here to characterise scaling rather than to introduce a new factor framework.
Doubling the merged \texttt{-persona} adapter roughly doubles every per-factor $\Delta$ with no obvious saturation; the under-scaled DPO+SFT soup attenuates everything correspondingly. Selectivity for F0 is similar in all three runs: F2 and F3 always shift more strongly than the target factor.
}
\label{tab:initiative-sup-scaling}
\end{table}
